title: ADA IC Product Profile
description: Technique-specific profile for Ion Chromatography (IC) products. Extends the base ADA product profile with constraints on valid IC component types.

allOf.title: Astromat Archive Product Metadata
allOf.description: Schema for JSON metadata documenting products in Astromat Data Archive (ADA). Each project consists of one or more data files and 0 to many supplemental files. Each file MUST have an associated YAML metadata file, with the same name, but '.yaml' as the file extension. Version 3.0 aligned with CDIF 2026 schema for DDI-CDI variable types and CSVW tabular data properties.

allOf.title: Core properties for CDIF metadata schema, with schema: prefixes
allOf.description: Building block specifies core properties for CDIF schema.org metadata record, applicable to any resource type.

@context.description: JSON-LD context declaring namespace prefixes used in the metadata record.

@id.description: The URI for the resource should be the @id value for the root of the JSON instance document tree. Must be an IRI (e.g. https://doi.org/..., urn:..., or a relative URI like #localid). Note that this identifier can be interpreted to identify the resource that is the subject of this metadata record, or the JSON-LD object that is the digital object containing the metadata information.

@type.description: a schema.org Class that specifies the expected information content for the metadata record. The array must include at least one schema.org type value. Default is schema:Dataset.

schema:name.description: A descriptive name of a dataset (e.g., 'Snow depth in Northern Hemisphere'). The name should uniquely identify the described resource for human use, in the scope of the metadata catalog containing this metadata record.

schema:description.description: A short summary describing a dataset. This text will be indexed by search applications, so the more information here, the better.

schema:identifier.description: The primary identifier for the dataset; other identifiers should be listed in the sameAs field. Schema.org has three ways of encoding identifiers-- a text description, a URL, or by using the schema:PropertyValue field. The Science on Schema.org guidance strongly recommends using the PropertyValue approach. see https://github.com/ESIPFed/science-on-schema.org   .... Dataset.md#identifier.  Ideally, for any given data provided they would provide identifiers either all as strings or all as identifier_type.

schema:additionalType.description: identifiers for datatypes from other vocabularies (not schema.org) that apply to this metadata.

schema:sameAs.description: Other identifiers for the dataset, as IRI references, literal strings, or structured identifiers using schema:PropertyValue.

@id.description: IRI for an equivalent resource or alternate identifier

schema:version.description: The version number or identifier for this dataset (text or numeric). The values should sort from oldest to newest using an alphanumeric sort on version strings

schema:inLanguage.description: the language of the dataset content

schema:dateModified.description: ISO8601 formatted date (and optional time if relevant) when Dataset was last updated

schema:datePublished.description: ISO8601 formatted date (and optional time if relevant) when Dataset was made public.

schema:conditionsOfAccess.description: text statement of conditions for use and access; if an online resource documents the restrictions or a URI is used to identify the conditions, recommend using schema:CreativeWork to provide a label (name) and an identifier (URI or URL).

@id.description: a reference to a resource defining conditions of Access

schema:license.description: legal statement of conditions for use and access; recommend using schema:CreativeWork to provide a label (name) for the license, and an identifier. Sources of license identifiers: https://opensource.org/licenses/, https://creativecommons.org/about/cclicenses/, https://spdx.org/licenses/, http://cor.esipfed.org/ont/earthcube/swl. If only a string is provided, it should be an identifier for the license, ideally a resolvable URI

@id.description: a reference to a license defintion

schema:url.description: Web Location of a page describing the dataset (landing page), typically providing links or instructions to get the actual resource content; analogous to dcat:accessURL. If a direct link is available to get the data, put in distribution/contentUrl

schema:distribution.description: specifies how to download the data in a specific format or access via a web API. This property describes where to get the data and in what format by using the schema:DataDownload type. If user must access data through a landing page, provide link to landing page in the 'url' property for the dataset

schema:relatedLink.description: links to related resources; linkRelationship specifies how the resource is related.

schema:publishingPrinciples.description: FDOF digitalObjectMutability, RDA digitalObjectPolicy, FDOF PersistencyPolicy. Policies related to maintenance, update, expected time to live. If an online resource documents the policies or a URI is used to identify the conditions, recommend using schema:CreativeWork to provide a label (name) and an identifier (URI or URL).

@id.description: a reference to a publishing principles statement

schema:keywords.description: Keywords are an array of strings, an array of schema:DefinedTerms, or some combination of these. If you have information about a controlled vocabulary from which keywords come from, use schema:DefinedTerm to descibe that keyword. This allowed variability makes parsing metadata hard; recommend using DefinedTerm for all keywords if any of them are from a known vocabulary, otherwise an array of strings.

schema:creator.description: author or orginator of intellectual content of dataset. Uset the JSON-LD @list construct to preserve author order. Use contributor with the Role property to specify other roles related to creation or stewardship of the resource.

@id.description: a identifier for an agent defined in this metadata, or externally; must be dereferenceable

schema:contributor.description: other parties who played a role in production of dataset

@id.description: a identifier for an agent defined in this metadata, or externally; must be dereferenceable

schema:publisher.description: Party who made the dataset publicly available

@id.description: a identifier for an agent defined in this metadata, or externally; must be dereferenceable

schema:provider.description: Party who maintains the distribution options for the dataset. If there are multiple distributions from different providers, use the provider property on distribution/DataDownload

@id.description: a identifier for an agent defined in this metadata, or externally; must be dereferenceable

prov:wasGeneratedBy.description: Brief information about instruments, software or experimental protocols used

prov:wasDerivedFrom.description: Brief information about sources of data used in aggregate datasets

allOf.title: CDIF Data Description properties
allOf.description: Additional constraints for CDIF data description level. Adds cdi:physicalDataType requirement on variableMeasured items and distribution-level cdi properties for file characterization (characterSet, fileSize, fileSizeUofM).

@id.description: uri for specifications that this metadata record conforms to

@context.description: Additional namespace prefix for data description properties.

allOf.description: All variableMeasured items at data description level must have an @id and cdi:physicalDataType so physical mappings can reference them.

@id.description: URI identifier for this variable, used as the target of cdi:formats_InstanceVariable references in physical mappings.

cdi:characterSet.description: The character set used in the distribution (e.g., UTF-8, ASCII).

cdi:fileSize.description: The size of the distribution file.

cdi:fileSizeUofM.description: Unit of measure for the file size (e.g., bytes, KB, MB, GB).

allOf.title: CDIF Archive Distribution
allOf.description: Building block that adds archive distribution as a valid distribution item type. cdifOptional already defines schema:distribution with DataDownload and WebAPI options; this building block extends that with the cdifArchive option (DataDownload with schema:hasPart component files).

@id.description: uri for specifications that this metadata record conforms to

allOf.title: CDIF Provenance
allOf.description: Building block that defines the prov:wasGeneratedBy property for CDIF metadata records. Wraps the cdifProvActivity building block as an array of provenance activities that describe how the described resource was generated.

@id.description: uri for specifications that this metadata record conforms to

prov:wasGeneratedBy.description: Provenance activities describing how the resource was generated, including agents, instruments, methodology, temporal bounds, and action chaining.

schema:additionalType.description: Should have the ada product type and 'ada:DataDeliveryPackage'

schema:license.description: Legal statement of conditions for use and access

anyOf.title: Creative Work Type
anyOf.description: Shell type for labeled links to other resources. Any schema.org CreativeWork property could be included.

schema:relatedLink.description: Links to related resources at the product level

schema:measurementTechnique.description: Text description of the measurement method

prov:wasGeneratedBy.description: ADA analysis events. Extends cdifProvActivity (from cdifProvenance) with ADA-specific instrument, laboratory, and sample properties.

items.title: ADA Analysis Instrument
items.description: ADA analytical instrument building block. Extends the core CDIF instrument building block with NeXus NXinstrument classification via additionalType. Instruments are typed as schema:Thing + schema:Product (from core) with domain-specific types (e.g. nxs:BaseClass/NXinstrument, ada:AMSInstrument) in additionalType.

allOf.title: Instrument Description
allOf.description: Schema for describing laboratory instruments and instrument systems. Supports ownership, manufacturer, model, commissioning dates, hierarchical sub-components via schema:hasPart, and domain-specific typing. Instruments may be standalone devices or complex systems with sub-components.

@id.description: Persistent identifier URI for this instrument metadata record.

@type.description: Must include schema:Product and schema:Thing. May include additional types.

schema:additionalType.description: Domain-specific type classifications. Should include a scientific instrument type URI (e.g. wd:Q3099911 for Wikidata scientific instrument).

schema:description.description: Text description of this instrument, its purpose, and capabilities.

schema:alternateName.description: Alternate name(s), e.g. specific make/model designation, common abbreviation.

schema:identifier.description: Formal identifier(s) for this instrument. Use PropertyValue for structured identifiers (e.g. PIDINST, serial number, inventory number).

schema:url.description: Landing page URL for this instrument.

schema:model.description: Product model information for this instrument.

schema:category.description: Instrument type classification(s) from a controlled vocabulary.

schema:contributor.description: Agents associated with this instrument in specific roles (e.g. operator, custodian, principal investigator). Use schema:owner for the owning organization.

schema:additionalProperty.description: Instrument-specific properties (measured variables, detection limits, calibration information, operating parameters, etc.).

schema:validThrough.description: Date the instrument was decommissioned (ISO 8601). Absent if still operational.

schema:hasPart.description: Sub-components of this instrument system. Each item is either an inline instrument description or an @id reference.

anyOf.description: A sub-component of an instrument system. Uses the same structure as the parent instrument but without nesting (single level of hasPart).

@id.description: Reference to a sub-component defined elsewhere.

schema:relatedLink.description: Links to related resources (manuals, datasheets, calibration records, related instruments).

schema:subjectOf.description: Catalog record metadata-about-metadata for this instrument description.

schema:dateModified.description: Date this metadata record was last modified (ISO 8601).

dcterms:conformsTo.description: Schema or profile this metadata record conforms to.

schema:additionalType.description: Domain-specific instrument type classifications. Should include a NeXus base class (e.g. nxs:BaseClass/NXinstrument) and/or technique-specific identifier (e.g. ada:AMSInstrument, GCMD instrument identifier).

schema:location.title: ADA Analysis Laboratory
schema:location.description: ADA laboratory or facility building block. Extends the core CDIF spatialExtent (schema:Place) with NeXus NXsource classification via additionalType. Inherits place name, identifier, alternateName, geo coordinates, and geosparql geometry from spatialExtent.

allOf.description: Spatial Extent description for CDIF discovery profile.  Note required prefix declaration for geoSparql ( http://www.opengis.net/ont/geosparql#) and OGC simple features (sf: http://www.opengis.net/ont/sf#)

schema:additionalType.description: Domain-specific type classifications for this place (e.g. facility type,laboratory classification, feature type).

schema:name.description: A place name for the location, either as a string or a DefinedTerm from a gazetteer.

schema:identifier.description: Formal identifier for this place (e.g. ROR identifier for a facility).

schema:alternateName.description: Alternate or abbreviated name(s) for this place.

schema:geo.description: Either a bounding box or a point location.

anyOf.description: A point location. Point locations are recommended for data that is associated with specific sample locations, particularly if these are widely spaced such that an enclosing bounding box would be a misleading representation of the spatial location. Be aware that some client applications might only index or display bounding box extents or a single point location.

A schema:GeoShape bounding box. The geometry is described with a set of latitude/longitude pairs (in that order).The documentation for schema:GeoShape states 'Either whitespace or commas can be used to separate latitude and longitude; whitespace should be used when writing a list of several such points.'

schema:box.description: A GeoShape box defines an area on the surface of the earth defined by point locations of the southwest corner and northeast corner of the rectangle in latitude-longitude coordinates. A space should be used to separate the latitude and longitude values. The two corner coordinate points are separated by a space. 'East longitude' means positive longitude values are east of the prime (Greenwich) meridian.

anyOf.description: A schema:GeoShape curvilinear trace, for resource related to a linear trace like a ship track or airplane flight line

schema:line.description: A GeoShape box defines a curvilinear geometry as a string of latitude-longitude coordinates. A space should be used to separate the latitude and longitude values. The coordinate point pairs are also separated by a space. 'East longitude' means positive longitude values are east of the prime (Greenwich) meridian. e.g. '39.33 120.77 40.44 123.96 41.00 121.34'

geosparql:hasGeometry.description: Optional geographic extent using wkt geometry, see Ocean InfoHub (https://book.oceaninfohub.org/thematics/spatial/README.html#simple-geosparql-wkt). Other geometry schemes might be specified in a specific domain profile, e.g. for atmospheric, subsurface data, or local coordinate systems. NOTE that the location specified here should be the same as the schema.org point or contained within the specified bounding box.

@type.description: MUST be sf: (<http://www.opengis.net/ont/sf#>) SimpleFeature geometry type. See https://opengeospatial.github.io/ogc-geosparql/geosparql11/sf_geometries.ttl

@value.description: a WKT geometry description string, e.g. 'POINT(-76 -18)'

@id.description: identifier string for coordinate reference system, e.g., 'CRS84'

schema:additionalType.description: Must include nxs:BaseClass/NXsource for NeXus facility classification.

schema:variableMeasured.description: ADA variable definitions. Extends cdifVariableMeasured (from cdifDataDescription) with additional ADA properties. Requires description with minLength 10.

items.description: Human intelligible name for variable that conveys semantics

Spatial Extent description for CDIF discovery profile.  Note required prefix declaration for geoSparql ( http://www.opengis.net/ont/geosparql#) and OGC simple features (sf: http://www.opengis.net/ont/sf#)

schema:additionalType.description: Domain-specific type classifications for this place (e.g. facility type,laboratory classification, feature type).

schema:name.description: A place name for the location, either as a string or a DefinedTerm from a gazetteer.

schema:identifier.description: Formal identifier for this place (e.g. ROR identifier for a facility).

schema:alternateName.description: Alternate or abbreviated name(s) for this place.

schema:geo.description: Either a bounding box or a point location.

anyOf.description: A point location. Point locations are recommended for data that is associated with specific sample locations, particularly if these are widely spaced such that an enclosing bounding box would be a misleading representation of the spatial location. Be aware that some client applications might only index or display bounding box extents or a single point location.

A schema:GeoShape bounding box. The geometry is described with a set of latitude/longitude pairs (in that order).The documentation for schema:GeoShape states 'Either whitespace or commas can be used to separate latitude and longitude; whitespace should be used when writing a list of several such points.'

schema:box.description: A GeoShape box defines an area on the surface of the earth defined by point locations of the southwest corner and northeast corner of the rectangle in latitude-longitude coordinates. A space should be used to separate the latitude and longitude values. The two corner coordinate points are separated by a space. 'East longitude' means positive longitude values are east of the prime (Greenwich) meridian.

anyOf.description: A schema:GeoShape curvilinear trace, for resource related to a linear trace like a ship track or airplane flight line

schema:line.description: A GeoShape box defines a curvilinear geometry as a string of latitude-longitude coordinates. A space should be used to separate the latitude and longitude values. The coordinate point pairs are also separated by a space. 'East longitude' means positive longitude values are east of the prime (Greenwich) meridian. e.g. '39.33 120.77 40.44 123.96 41.00 121.34'

geosparql:hasGeometry.description: Optional geographic extent using wkt geometry, see Ocean InfoHub (https://book.oceaninfohub.org/thematics/spatial/README.html#simple-geosparql-wkt). Other geometry schemes might be specified in a specific domain profile, e.g. for atmospheric, subsurface data, or local coordinate systems. NOTE that the location specified here should be the same as the schema.org point or contained within the specified bounding box.

@type.description: MUST be sf: (<http://www.opengis.net/ont/sf#>) SimpleFeature geometry type. See https://opengeospatial.github.io/ogc-geosparql/geosparql11/sf_geometries.ttl

@value.description: a WKT geometry description string, e.g. 'POINT(-76 -18)'

@id.description: identifier string for coordinate reference system, e.g., 'CRS84'

items.description: The time interval during which data was collected or observations were made; or a time period that an activity or collection is linked to intellectually or thematically (for example, 1997 to 1998; the 18th century) (see https://documentation.ardc.edu.au/display/DOC/Temporal+coverage). For documentation of Earth Science, Paleobiology or Paleontology datasets, we are interested in the second case-- the time period that data are linked to thematically. NOTE-- context must include "time": "http://www.w3.org/2006/time#"}

anyOf.description: a w3c time proper interval with bounds that are named time ordinal eras with identifier, e.g. geologic age. This is a SOSO schema.org extension

schema:description.description: free text description of the temporal interval

time:intervalStartedBy.description: identifier for a named time ordinal era that is older bound of time interval, e.g. 'isc:LowerDevonian'

time:intervalFinishedBy.description: identifier for a named time ordinal era that is younger bound of time interval, e.g. 'isc:LowerDevonian'

anyOf.description: a w3c time proper interval with bounds that numeric ages.

@context.description: Must exactly match the specified @context object.

schema:description.description: free text description of the temporal interval

time:hasTRS.description: identifier for a temporal reference system; default is million years before prsent as a decimal number
identifier for a temporal reference system; default is million years before prsent as a decimal number

anyOf.description: Simple ISO8601 encoding of calendar date, dateTime, or time interval with calendar date bounds

dqv:hasQualityMeasurement.description: Quality measurements reported to assess the resource.

items.title: definitions for simple quality measure property

dqv:isMeasurementOf.description: specify the quality measure that is reported, by name, with an ID ref, or as a Defined Term

@id.description: a resolvable reference to a representation of a quality measure

dqv:value.description: the reported result of the quality measure, either as a string or a defined term from a vocabulary

schema:distribution.description: Distribution inherits from cdifCore (DataDownload, WebAPI) and cdifArchiveDistribution (archive with hasPart). ADA file-type classification uses ada:componentType on hasPart items.

allOf.title: Files Type
allOf.description: Properties for any file in an ADA product distribution hasPart. These are component files within an archive — they are NOT individually downloadable (no schema:contentUrl). The @type must NOT include schema:DataDownload. GeneralType provides info based on broad categories of file format (tabular, image, dataCube, document).
Base properties common to all file types in hasPart

schema:additionalType.description: Other classifiers for the file. ADA componentTypes are specified in the specific file type schemas attached by $refs.

schema:name.description: String name of file, must be unique within the containing package.

schema:relatedLink.description: Links between files in the product. Use schema:name for path to target in product, or use #id JSON-LD links. Used to link metadata files in bundle to the data or supplementary files they document.

anyOf.title: Image Type
anyOf.description: Image objects with acquisition details and component type classification. Typed as ada:image and schema:ImageObject.

ada:illuminationType.description: Type of illumination used to create the image. Examples include Visible light, Cross-polarized visible light, ultraviolet light, Electron beam, X-ray.

ada:imageType.description: Specifies the nature of the sample's response to the illumination that was detected and measured.

anyOf.title: Image Map Type
anyOf.description: Spatially registered image maps with pixel coordinates, component type classification, and spatial registration metadata.

anyOf.title: EMPA Instrument Detail
anyOf.description: Electron Microprobe Analysis with spectrometer and signal details.

ada:illuminationType.description: Type of illumination used to create the image.

ada:imageType.description: Specifies the nature of the sample's response to the illumination.

ada:spatialRegistration.title: Spatial Registration Type
ada:spatialRegistration.description: Defines pixel coordinate system registration including origin coordinates, pixel scales, and coordinate definition type for spatially registered images and maps.

ada:basemap.description: link to appropriate basemap image map

ada:coordDef.description: Whether coordinates are stage-defined or pixel-defined. If pixel-defined, are coordinates from stage, upperleftPixel, or centerPixel.

ada:originLocation.description: The location of the origin pixel of an image. Range: upperLeft, upperRight, lowerLeft, lowerRight, center

anyOf.title: Tabular Data Type
anyOf.description: Tabular data type aligned with CDIF 2026 schema using DDI-CDI and CSVW properties. Typed as cdi:TabularTextDataSet and ada:tabularData.

anyOf.title: DSC Instrument Detail
anyOf.description: Differential Scanning Calorimetry heat tabular data

anyOf.title: EA-IRMS Instrument Detail
anyOf.description: Elemental Analysis Isotope Ratio Mass Spectrometry collection

anyOf.title: EMPA Instrument Detail
anyOf.description: Electron Microprobe Analysis with spectrometer and signal details.

anyOf.title: LAF Instrument Detail
anyOf.description: Laser Ablation Fluorescence processed/raw data. elementAnalyzed goes in resultTarget.

anyOf.title: NanoSIMS Instrument Detail
anyOf.description: Nano Secondary Ion Mass Spectrometry with isotope tracking

anyOf.title: NanoIR Instrument Detail
anyOf.description: Nano-IR spectroscopy collections with phase analysis

anyOf.title: PSFD Instrument Detail
anyOf.description: Point Spread Function Data with image names and conditions

anyOf.title: VNMIR Instrument Detail
anyOf.description: Very-Near Mid-IR spectroscopy with detailed measurement parameters

anyOf.title: XRD Instrument Detail
anyOf.description: X-ray Diffraction tabular data with geometry and wavelength

ada:xCoordCol.description: The column names are redundant, they are lists in the hasPhysicalMapping array. Include here for convenience.

ada:spatialRegistration.title: Spatial Registration Type
ada:spatialRegistration.description: Defines pixel coordinate system registration including origin coordinates, pixel scales, and coordinate definition type for spatially registered images and maps.

ada:basemap.description: link to appropriate basemap image map

ada:coordDef.description: Whether coordinates are stage-defined or pixel-defined. If pixel-defined, are coordinates from stage, upperleftPixel, or centerPixel.

ada:originLocation.description: The location of the origin pixel of an image. Range: upperLeft, upperRight, lowerLeft, lowerRight, center

allOf.title: Tabular Data Type
allOf.description: Tabular data type aligned with CDIF 2026 schema using DDI-CDI and CSVW properties. Typed as cdi:TabularTextDataSet and ada:tabularData.

csvw:commentPrefix.description: An atomic property that sets the comment prefix flag to the single provided value, which MUST be a string. The default is '#'.

csvw:delimiter.description: Sets the delimiter flag to the single provided value, which MUST be a string. The default is ','.

csvw:header.description: If true, sets the header row count flag to 1, and if false to 0, unless headerRowCount is provided, in which case the value provided for the header property is ignored. The default is true.

csvw:headerRowCount.description: A numeric atomic property that sets the header row count flag to the single provided value, which MUST be a non-negative integer.

cdi:isDelimited.description: Schema constraint is that one of {'isDelimited','isFixedWidth'} must be present with a True value; the other one may be present or omitted, but if present must have a false value.

csvw:quoteChar.description: Same as DDI-CDI quoteCharacter. The string that is used around escaped cells.

csvw:skipColumns.description: The number of columns to skip at the beginning of each row.

csvw:skipRows.description: The number of rows to skip at the beginning of the file, before a header row or tabular data.

cdi:escapeCharacter.description: The character used to escape special characters in the data. From DDI-CDI PhysicalSegmentLayout.escapeCharacter.

cdi:headerIsCaseSensitive.description: Whether column header names are case-sensitive. From DDI-CDI PhysicalSegmentLayout.headerIsCaseSensitive.

cdi:treatConsecutiveDelimitersAsOne.description: Whether consecutive delimiters should be treated as a single delimiter. From DDI-CDI PhysicalSegmentLayout.treatConsecutiveDelimitersAsOne.

csvw:tableDirection.description: Indicates the direction of the table layout (left-to-right or right-to-left). From DDI-CDI PhysicalSegmentLayout.tableDirection.

csvw:textDirection.description: Indicates whether the text within cells should be displayed as left-to-right, right-to-left, according to content, or inherited from the table direction. From DDI-CDI PhysicalSegmentLayout.textDirection.

csvw:trim.description: Indicates whether to trim whitespace around cells. 'true' corresponds to DDI-CDI 'both' value, 'false' corresponds to DDI-CDI 'neither' value.

cdi:hasPhysicalMapping.description: Links variables to their physical representation in this dataset.

anyOf.title: Collection Type
anyOf.description: A collection can be a set of files with identical information model and serialization/formatting, or a heterogeneous set of files that together constitute a dataset (a composite dataset).

ada:memberTypes.title: String Array Type
ada:memberTypes.description: List of the component types in the collection

ada:nFiles.description: Number of files in the collection, including metadata files

ada:fileName.description: Full path to file in container object

ada:fileNamePattern.description: Pattern for collection members with differentiating suffix at '*'

ada:componentType.description: The component type for the file(s)

anyOf.title: Data Cube Type
anyOf.description: Multi-dimensional data cube structure using DDI-CDI. Typed as ada:dataCube and cdi:StructuredDataSet per CDIF 2026 alignment.

anyOf.title: L2MS Instrument Detail
anyOf.description: Laser-2 Mass Spectrometry cube data with ionization parameters

allOf.title: Data Cube Type
allOf.description: Multi-dimensional data cube structure using DDI-CDI. Typed as ada:dataCube and cdi:StructuredDataSet per CDIF 2026 alignment.

cdi:hasPhysicalMapping.description: Links variables to their physical representation in this dataset.

cdi:locator.description: String that can be used by software to locate values of the variable in this physical dataset.

anyOf.title: Document Type
anyOf.description: Text or PDF/A documents providing supplemental information, typically related to calibration, instrument metadata details, analysis methods, or data representation. Typed as ada:document and schema:DigitalDocument.

ada:componentType.description: One of the supplemental document types if applicable

anyOf.title: ARGT Instrument Detail
anyOf.description: ARGT (Argon) document type with phase and isotope analysis

anyOf.title: Supplemental Document Image Type
anyOf.description: Supplemental document images including analysis location images, annotated products, context photography, and other supplemental visual materials.

schema:isBasedOn.description: Original file name before PDS4-compliant renaming

anyOf.title: Other File Type
anyOf.description: Files in other widely-used formats approved for submission to the Astromat Archive. Includes EMSA, OBJ, STL, XLSX, and Neptune Plus export formats.

anyOf.title: SLS Instrument Detail
anyOf.description: Structured Light Scanning shape models and partial scans

schema:encodingFormat.description: One of the approved non-standard file formats: 'Spectral Data Exchange File (.emsa)'-->text/plain; '3D model file (.obj)'-->model/obj; 'Standard Triangle Language (.stl)'-->model/stl; 'Open XML workbook (.xlsx)'-->application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.spreadsheetml.sheet; 'Neptune Plus export (.exp)'-->application/octet-stream

ada:formatDescription.description: Free text explanation of file format, or a link to a publicly accessible specification for the format.

anyOf.description: Generic media object (fallback for unrecognized file types)

ada:componentType.description: ADA file type classification. Values are restricted by technique-specific profiles. Universal types (e.g. ada:calibrationFile, ada:report) are valid across all techniques.

schema:additionalType.description: Must include a IC product type identifier.

schema:distribution.description: Distribution items for adaIC. Archive hasPart items must have ada:componentType from technique-specific or universal values.

anyOf.description: Component type values valid across all ADA techniques. Used in ada:componentType on hasPart items.
